title: Bridge Essay: Cerebellar Contributions to Social Cognition in ASD: A Predictive Processing Framework.
concept: Intersubjectivity, embodied empathy, social predictive processing.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Social Incarnation** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Social Incarnation — intersubjectivity, embodied empathy, social predictive processing.
**Phase context:** Intersubjectivity, embodied empathy, social predictive processing.

**Cerebellar Contributions to Social Cognition in ASD: A Predictive Processing Framework.** — Front Integr Neurosci (2022). DOI: 10.3389/fnint.2022.810425. PMID: 35153691. Functional, structural, and cytoarchitectural differences in the cerebellum are consistently reported in Autism Spectrum Disorders (ASD). Despite this, the mechanisms governing cerebellar contributions to ASD, particularly within the sociocognitive domain, are not well understood.
